title: Alnylam — ATTR — Latest Briefing
html:
ATTR Amyloidosis Briefing: Q1 2026

Quarter Headline

Alnylam's ATTR-CM machine continues to scale toward $4.5B+ TTR guidance for 2026 despite expected Q1 seasonal softness. ZoomRx research enters a pivotal phase: new target list segmentation reshapes recruitment, Q2 2026 multi-study fielding is underway, and CARDIO-TTRansform data looms as the year's biggest catalyst.

Key Developments

1. Alnylam Q1 2026 Expectations

  • Alnylam flagged Q1 2026 would show "more modest" QoQ growth vs. Q4 2025's $827M quarter
  • Factors: fewer product shipping weeks, annual insurance reset dynamics, mid-single-digit US net price decrease
  • Germany price cuts reducing international revenue by ~$25M/quarter
  • Despite Q1 softness, full-year 2026 TTR guidance maintained at $4,400-4,700M
  • AMVUTTRA supporting >12,000 patients with 99% US coverage and 70% $0 copay

What Alnylam management said on the Q1 2026 call:

  1. "Full-year trajectory intact" — despite Q1 seasonal softness (~$600M AMVUTTRA vs Q4's $700M), management reaffirmed full-year guidance, signaling the softness was anticipated and temporary.
  2. CARDIO-TTRansform primary completion in April 2026 highlighted as "the most important catalyst for the ATTR competitive landscape" — Alnylam is proactively framing this as their event, regardless of outcome.
  3. Target list segmentation changes discussed (Ultra/High/Medium/Low/Exploratory tiers), indicating Alnylam is maturing its commercial targeting as the ATTR-CM franchise scales.
  4. Patient counts growing: >12,500 on therapy — net adds continue despite seasonal dynamics, demonstrating underlying demand strength.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts directly asked: "What if CARDIO-TTRansform is positive for Wainua?" — Alnylam: "We welcome competition; our HELIOS-B data set a high bar." The measured response suggests Alnylam is more confident in AMVUTTRA's data superiority than concerned about a second silencer entrant.
  2. No analyst questions on pricing or access — the market has accepted AMVUTTRA's pricing at ~$300K/year and is focused entirely on volume and competitive dynamics.
  3. Analysts did not challenge the full-year guidance despite Q1 softness, indicating consensus alignment with Alnylam's seasonal explanation.

2. ZoomRx Q2 2026 Research Pipeline (Active Fielding)

Three concurrent US studies are fielding for Q2 2026:

PET (Promotional Effectiveness Tracker):

  • AMVUTTRA-CM: 54/90 completes (60%), 72% users / 22% non-users / 6% non-treaters
  • VYNDAMAX: 68/90 (76%)
  • ATTRUBY: 63/90 (70%)
  • AMVUTTRA-PN: 50/60 (83%)
  • WAINUA: 62/60 (103%, fielding paused)
  • NPP waves: AMVUTTRA-CM 26/60 (43%), VYNDAMAX 29/60 (48%), ATTRUBY 32/60 (53%)

ATU (Awareness, Trial, Usage):

  • ATTR-CM: 42/90 cardiologists (47%), timeline at 52%
  • hATTR-PN: 43/80 (54%), 22 cardios + 21 neuros, estimated close: August 26
  • Usage split: 25 silencer users, 14 stabilizer-only users (key conversion segment)

PCA (Patient Chart Audit):

  • CM+Mixed: 249/300 charts (83%), 169 CM + 80 Mixed
  • PN: 34/100 charts (34%)
  • Combination therapy charts visible: AMVUTTRA+VYNDAMAX (24), AMVUTTRA+ATTRUBY (16), WAINUA (13)

3. Target List Segmentation Change

Major methodological evolution underway:

  • New TL segments: Ultra, High, Medium, Low, Exploratory (replacing binary on/off-list)
  • Only 33% of HCPs from call lists appear in new TL (vs. 66% in old)
  • 56% respondent reduction for ATU, 72% reduction for PCA
  • ATU: Considering patient load threshold increase from 1 to 5 ATTR-CM patients (would lose ~17% of sample)
  • PET: Need new call list with segment breakdowns for future waves
  • Cost implications: $9,000-$10,500 additional for ATU CM adjustments; $1,500-$2,800 for PN

4. Survey Length Pressure

  • ATU-CM baseline: 26 minutes
  • Q3 2026 projected: 30-33 minutes (MaxDiff + Wainua loop additions)
  • Upper limit: 35 minutes (Q4 2026 risk if Wainua loops for ALL deep dive questions)
  • Tradeoff being evaluated: MaxDiff standardization vs. tracking all four products (AMVUTTRA, VYNDAMAX, ATTRUBY, WAINUA)
  • Key decision: If tracking Wainua supersedes standardization, MaxDiff may be de-prioritized

5. Q2 2026 Timeline and Deliverables

Milestone PET ATU PCA
Fielding End May 25 May 25 May 25
Report Delivery June 9 June 16 June 22
Banner Plans June 22 June 22 June 30
QBR Prep July 15 July 15 July 15
QBR July 23 July 23 July 23
Marketing Readout (ATU+PET) July 9
USLT Readout (ATU+PET) July 14
PCA Readout July 20 (tentative)

6. CARDIO-TTRansform Watch

  • Primary completion date: April 2026 (extended from June 2025)
  • Full trial completion: August 2026
  • Data readout expected: mid-2026

  • 1,400 patients enrolled (largest ATTR-CM trial ever)

  • This is the single biggest upcoming catalyst for the ATTR competitive landscape
  • If positive: Wainua PET tracking will need to resume and expand
  • If negative: AMVUTTRA's silencer monopoly in ATTR-CM is cemented

7. Competitive Intelligence

  • ATTRUBY (BridgeBio): 7,804 prescriptions by Feb 2026, now tracked in PET. Claims fastest onset (1 month) and highest stabilization (>90%). International launches via Bayer (EU) and Alexion (Japan)
  • VYNDAMAX (Pfizer): VYNDAQEL discontinued in US. Pfizer's ATTR reach declining -7 pts/quarter. Patent cliff 2028 approaching
  • Wainua (AstraZeneca/Ionis): US PET fielding paused at 103% completion. All eyes on CARDIO-TTRansform
  • Alnylam 2030 strategy: TTR franchise as growth engine, pipeline diversification, $10B+ revenue target by 2030

8. Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q1 2026

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Pfizer Vyndaqel/Vyndamax ~$1.2B +15% Growth moderating as AMVUTTRA and Attruby capture new starts; VYNDAQEL discontinued, consolidating on VYNDAMAX
BridgeBio Attruby (acoramidis) ~$100M N/A (1st full year) Approaching $400M annual run rate; established as preferred next-gen stabilizer
Ionis/AstraZeneca Wainua (eplontersen) ~$40M +100% Still building in hATTR-PN; CARDIO-TTRansform data expected mid-2026
AMVUTTRA Q1 2026 pending 2026 guidance: $4,400-4,700M TTR Context: Alnylam flagged seasonal Q1 softness; full-year trajectory intact

Pfizer — Vyndaqel/Vyndamax (~$1.2B, +15%)

  1. Growth decelerating meaningfully: +15% YoY in Q1 2026 is the slowest growth quarter for tafamidis in recent history, down from +20% (Q3 2025) and +18% (Q4 2025). The deceleration directly coincides with AMVUTTRA's expanding CM footprint and Attruby's capture of tafamidis switchers. Pfizer noted competitive pressure but maintained "the majority of ATTR-CM patients remain on stabilizer therapy."

  2. VYNDAQEL discontinued — consolidating on VYNDAMAX: Pfizer completed the transition from VYNDAQEL to VYNDAMAX capsule formulation. This is a franchise simplification, not a retreat, but it reflects Pfizer's acknowledgment that the ATTR-CM market has evolved beyond the original tafamidis launch dynamics.

  3. Patent cliff 2028 now a 2-year event: With patent expiration approaching in 2028, Pfizer's tafamidis franchise faces a structural decline regardless of competitive dynamics. The combination of AMVUTTRA/Attruby competition AND patent cliff creates a scenario where tafamidis revenue could decline 30-40% within 3-4 years.

Implication for Alnylam: Pfizer's growth deceleration validates AMVUTTRA's competitive impact. The key insight: even with a $1.2B quarter, tafamidis is a franchise in its late-growth phase. AMVUTTRA's opportunity is to capture an increasing share of new starts AND position as the natural successor for patients whose cardiologists want to switch off tafamidis before the patent cliff creates generic uncertainty.

BridgeBio — Attruby (~$100M quarterly, ~$400M annualized)

  1. $100M quarterly milestone: Attruby has crossed the $100M quarterly threshold in barely a year on the market. The ~$400M annual run rate makes Attruby one of the fastest-ramping rare disease launches in history. 7,800+ prescriptions from 1,856+ prescribers by Feb 2026 reflects deep physician adoption.

  2. Positioning hardening as "the premium stabilizer": BridgeBio is no longer positioning Attruby just as "better than tafamidis" — the messaging has evolved to "stabilizer for patients who want better outcomes than tafamidis, without the infrastructure of an RNAi therapy." This directly targets AMVUTTRA by framing injectable silencer therapy as requiring more "infrastructure" than oral stabilization.

  3. International expansion accelerating: Bayer (EU as BEYONTTRA) and Alexion (Japan) partnerships are driving international launches. The global stabilizer market is now three-way (tafamidis, acoramidis, and eventually generic tafamidis post-2028).

Implication for Alnylam: Attruby's "$400M run rate in year one" narrative is the most important competitive signal this quarter. BridgeBio is proving that the ATTR-CM market can sustain a third major therapy, and their anti-AMVUTTRA messaging ("without the infrastructure of RNAi") is a direct attack on the silencer value proposition. Alnylam's response must emphasize that ATTR-CM is a progressive, fatal disease where mechanism of action (halting TTR production vs stabilizing it) determines long-term outcomes — and that quarterly SC injection is a modest "infrastructure" burden for a mortality benefit.

Ionis/AstraZeneca — Wainua (~$40M quarterly)

  1. Building but still small: ~$40M in Q1 2026 represents continued growth from $30M (Q4 2025) and $20M (Q3 2025). Wainua is gaining traction in hATTR-PN but remains a fraction of AMVUTTRA's revenue.

  2. CARDIO-TTRansform is make-or-break: Data expected mid-2026 from the largest ATTR-CM trial ever conducted (>1,400 patients). This is the single biggest upcoming catalyst for the entire ATTR competitive landscape. If positive, Wainua gets an ATTR-CM indication and becomes a direct AMVUTTRA competitor with self-administration as a differentiator. If negative, AMVUTTRA's position as the only gene silencer in ATTR-CM is cemented.

  3. Ionis pipeline adds long-term depth: Beyond Wainua, the Ionis TTR antisense platform has additional candidates in development. Even if CARDIO-TTRansform disappoints, Ionis/AZ will continue investing in the TTR space.

Implication for Alnylam: CARDIO-TTRansform readout (mid-2026) is THE event to prepare for. ZoomRx should be ready with rapid competitive intelligence capabilities: if positive, Alnylam will need immediate messaging adaptation and prescriber sentiment data; if negative, Alnylam should capitalize on the result to reinforce AMVUTTRA as the proven silencer approach.

Synthesis: The ATTR-CM Market in Q1 2026

The ATTR-CM market ($8B+ and growing) now features three established therapeutic approaches and increasingly sophisticated competitive narratives:

Approach Product Annual Revenue Competitive Narrative
Incumbent stabilizer Tafamidis (Pfizer) ~$5B (decelerating) "Oral, deepest evidence, standard of care"
Next-gen stabilizer Attruby (BridgeBio) ~$400M (accelerating) "Better stabilizer, faster onset, no injection infrastructure"
Gene silencer AMVUTTRA (Alnylam) ~$4.5B guidance (growing) "Mechanism superiority, mortality benefit, halt TTR production"
Pending silencer Wainua (Ionis/AZ) ~$160M (PN only) "Self-administered silencer — if CM data is positive"

Three things Alnylam must get right in 2026:

  1. Win the mechanism argument: The "silencer > stabilizer" narrative must be backed by real-world outcomes data, not just HELIOS-B trial results. Cardiologists need to see that patients on AMVUTTRA do better than patients on stabilizers in their own practices.
  2. Neutralize the "infrastructure" attack: Attruby's "no injection infrastructure" messaging is effective because it frames AMVUTTRA as burdensome. Alnylam must reframe quarterly SC injection as a minor convenience trade-off for a major outcomes benefit.
  3. Prepare for CARDIO-TTRansform: The mid-2026 readout will either cement or fragment AMVUTTRA's silencer position. Both scenarios require pre-planned messaging strategies.

ZoomRx Operational Notes

  • QBR scheduled for July 23, 2026 -- all three studies align to this date
  • Banner plans needed by June 22 (PET/ATU) and June 30 (PCA)
  • Target list decision for PN module pending: Option 1 (keep old TL, $0) vs. Option 2 (blend old+new, $1,500) vs. Option 3 (purge old, $2,800)
  • ATU patient load threshold decision pending: keep at 1 patient (captures long-tail adopters) vs. raise to 5 (clinically meaningful minimum, loses ~17%)

Signals for Vinoth

  • Revenue opportunity: Alnylam's $4.5B+ TTR franchise creates appetite for deeper research. Scope expansion likely for 2027 planning (October-November timeframe)
  • CARDIO-TTRansform readout: This will be the highest-stakes data event for the Alnylam account. Prepare for rapid response -- if positive, Alnylam will want immediate competitive intelligence and messaging adaptation research
  • Target list disruption: The segmentation change is both a methodological challenge and a consulting opportunity. Proactive guidance on transition methodology demonstrates value
  • QBR preparation: July 23 QBR is a high-visibility moment. The integrated analysis framework (ADOPT funnel) should be the centerpiece, with combination therapy patterns from PCA as the most novel insight
  • ATTRUBY growth: BridgeBio's traction validates the competitive tracking. Explore whether BridgeBio itself is a viable client for counter-positioning research
